 Hi Dina,
 
 As mentioned on our calls, I wanted to share some profiles from our early candidate outreach. These are strong standouts who check the boxes on the must-haves and come directly from the target competitors. It’s worth noting that these are the ones who have been willing to engage with us so far (and are the better ones), which makes them especially relevant data points in terms of both experience and market compensation.
 
 Candidate 1 – Michael Daddis

 * Worked for: Arrow Electronics, Carestream, Flygt US
 * Experience: 30+ years in inside sales with only 2.5 with Arrow; currently ISR at Arrow handling top-tier accounts ($500K+ annual spend). He is responsible for quoting, closing sales, expediting, and forecasting. He mentioned he does not do a lot of engineering but will sometimes specify parts for customers.
 * Compensation: $72K base, 60/40 split, ~$120K OTE. Notes that Tevet’s current range would fall below his base. 

 Candidate 2 – Janna Shepard

 * Worked for: Honeywell, Arrow Electronics
 * Experience: 2–3 years in inside sales with target competitors; closed $1.5M in four months at Honeywell.
 * Compensation: Prior base $90K at Honeywell + 20% incentive; ~$60–70K at Arrow with 60/40 split. Current target OTE ~$100K. Feels Tevet’s offer is “really low.”
 * She has sold items such as meters and sensors, which are the same types of products Tevet sells. She closed $1.5 million in business in four months at Honeywell, with her largest deal being close to $200k. She also has experience with CRM platforms. She has a hunter mentality and is motivated by winning

 Candidate 3 – Amanda Fox

 * Worked for: Arrow Electronics (current ISR), Opus IVS, H.H. Barnum
 * Experience: 6+ years inside sales, 9+ years total sales; $2M+ quarterly quota at Arrow; handles million-dollar accounts.
 * Compensation: ~$63K base, ~$105K OTE. Would need to maintain current earnings and is only open to the higher end of Tevet’s range ($75K).
 * Amanda has over two years of inside sales experience. Although she is not in the Aerospace and Defense division at Arrow, she is familiar with how it operates and knows that there are different parameters for it due to confidentiality. Amanda has a quarterly sales goal of just over $2 million and works with accounts that include a customer who spends over $1 million a year, demonstrating her ability to handle complex deals and high-value accounts. 

 Overall key takeaways so far:

 * Even junior candidates from target competitors are earning total comp well above Tevet’s $60–75K range. The real driver in candidate conversations is OTE, not base salary. The market “sweet spot” appears to be $100–110K OTE. Candidates are asking whether Tevet’s plan would realistically allow them to hit that level. Confirming whether these candidates could get there is going to be critical.
 * Finding more junior people without defense/government experience may be one option, but as Amanda’s case shows, even those profiles are still sitting at ~$105K OTE.
 * This could become a tricky battle if the range remains unchanged, as we’ll be filtering for a very small select number of people with strictly 2-3 years of experience who are both available and willing to take a step down in pay. Options to consider:

 * Increasing compensation to align with the market particularly on OTE/commissions. 
 * Reducing some of the DoD/industry-specific requirements to expand the candidate pool.

 Lastly, could you clarify how the commission plan works in detail? I recalled the team confirming that it is uncapped. But if someone exceeds 100% attainment, how much could they realistically earn? The more we can position the upside to candidates, the better chance we’ll have of attracting top talent.
 
 Let me know if it is easier to jump on a call to discuss further based on this initial feedback and questions we are seeing.

 

 Thank you!

 Hi Dina,
 
 As mentioned on our calls, I wanted to share some profiles from our early candidate outreach. These are strong standouts who check the boxes on the must-haves and come directly from the target competitors. It’s worth noting that these are the ones who have been willing to engage with us so far (and are the better ones), which makes them especially relevant data points in terms of both experience and market compensation.
 
 Candidate 1 – Michael Daddis

 * Worked for: Arrow Electronics, Carestream, Flygt US
 * Experience: 30+ years in inside sales with only 2.5 with Arrow; currently ISR at Arrow handling top-tier accounts ($500K+ annual spend). He is responsible for quoting, closing sales, expediting, and forecasting. He mentioned he does not do a lot of engineering but will sometimes specify parts for customers.
 * Compensation: $72K base, 60/40 split, ~$120K OTE. Notes that Tevet’s current range would fall below his base. 

 Candidate 2 – Janna Shepard

 * Worked for: Honeywell, Arrow Electronics
 * Experience: 2–3 years in inside sales with target competitors; closed $1.5M in four months at Honeywell.
 * Compensation: Prior base $90K at Honeywell + 20% incentive; ~$60–70K at Arrow with 60/40 split. Current target OTE ~$100K. Feels Tevet’s offer is “really low.”
 * She has sold items such as meters and sensors, which are the same types of products Tevet sells. She closed $1.5 million in business in four months at Honeywell, with her largest deal being close to $200k. She also has experience with CRM platforms. She has a hunter mentality and is motivated by winning

 Candidate 3 – Amanda Fox

 * Worked for: Arrow Electronics (current ISR), Opus IVS, H.H. Barnum
 * Experience: 6+ years inside sales, 9+ years total sales; $2M+ quarterly quota at Arrow; handles million-dollar accounts.
 * Compensation: ~$63K base, ~$105K OTE. Would need to maintain current earnings and is only open to the higher end of Tevet’s range ($75K).
 * Amanda has over two years of inside sales experience. Although she is not in the Aerospace and Defense division at Arrow, she is familiar with how it operates and knows that there are different parameters for it due to confidentiality. Amanda has a quarterly sales goal of just over $2 million and works with accounts that include a customer who spends over $1 million a year, demonstrating her ability to handle complex deals and high-value accounts. 

 Overall key takeaways so far:

 * Even junior candidates from target competitors are earning total comp well above Tevet’s $60–75K range. The real driver in candidate conversations is OTE, not base salary. The market “sweet spot” appears to be $100–110K OTE. Candidates are asking whether Tevet’s plan would realistically allow them to hit that level. Confirming whether these candidates could get there is going to be critical.
 * Finding more junior people without defense/government experience may be one option, but as Amanda’s case shows, even those profiles are still sitting at ~$105K OTE.
 * This could become a tricky battle if the range remains unchanged, as we’ll be filtering for a very small select number of people with strictly 2-3 years of experience who are both available and willing to take a step down in pay. Options to consider:

 * Increasing compensation to align with the market particularly on OTE/commissions. 
 * Reducing some of the DoD/industry-specific requirements to expand the candidate pool.

 Lastly, could you clarify how the commission plan works in detail? I recalled the team confirming that it is uncapped. But if someone exceeds 100% attainment, how much could they realistically earn? The more we can position the upside to candidates, the better chance we’ll have of attracting top talent.
 
 Let me know if it is easier to jump on a call to discuss further based on this initial feedback and questions we are seeing.
